Title: Am I Home?
Credits: Created by Adam Smith (amsmith@ucsc.edu). Edited by Trinity Willis

Back door.Body: You face a plain, white door. It's unassuming, familiar but unknown. Are there some new scratches in the wood? You haven't been here in years so it's hard to tell.

Laundry Room.Body: A laundry room. Simple, neat, functional. Two litter boxes sit to the left and you wonder where the cats might be. You take off your shoes and set them on the shoe rack in smooth, practiced motions.

Kitchen.Body: It's a large kitchen with an island in the middle. The walls are hand painted a mix of oranges and yellows with sunflowers dancing along near the ceiling. A few strands of pasta stick to the roof above the stove from spaghetti dinners long past.

Office.Body: You walk through a large arch into the office. A white desk against a line of windows holds the remnants of various projects and papers. Bookshelves line the walls filled to the brim with art supplies.

Living Room.Body: In the living room a line of bookshelves piled with books and CDs sit behind two plush sofas with swiring threaded patterns. A fat black cat naps in a cat tree by the front door.

InteractiveObject.Body: She looks up when you approach and purrs anticipatedly.

Choices.Result: The cat purrs and leans into your hand. Her tail flicks contentedly. You remember how you found her in the backyard sleeping in the dry grass under the lemon tree. You remember how skinny she was back then, how you left food and blankets out for her. You remember the first time she came inside and was so tired she slept for days. You named her Polly. When she rolls over onto her back and looks up at you you can see the trust in her eyes.

Hallway.Body: Pictures line the hallway walls. A collage of 12 school pictures, some family pictures, a few kid's paintings. You remember sprinting down this hall many times.

Back Door.Body: The back door leads out onto a covered patio. Dry, rocky soil marks an empty path down the side of the house, broken up only by a large lemon tree.

Guest Bathroom.Body: It's a simple bathroom. Clean, white, well maintained.

Guest Bedroom.Body: This was your room once. It's the cat room now. A cat tree sits next to some spare food and litter. A gray cat sits on the windowsill and watches hummingbirds on a feeder outside.

InteractiveObject.Body: The cat turns his head when you approach. When he notices you he jumps to his feet and starts meowing, coming to circle around your feet and rub against your legs.

Choices.Result: You remember when you picked him out from his litter at the shelter. He was only a few months old, tiny, and fluffier than his siblings. He was named Colonel after Colonel Mustard from the board game Clue. He had always been your cat, sleeping by your feet at night and sitting on your windowsill while you studied at your desk. He recognizes you when you visit, no matter how long it's been.

Your Room.Body: It's a cluttered, lived in space. Some well-loved stuffed animals sit atop a loft bed. The desk underneath is covered in papers and books. Bookshelves are stocked with knick knacks. It's exactly how you remember it.

Mom's Room.Body: Mom preferred the guest bedroom. This room was too big, too open. Her hand painted dresser sits againts the wall. Two walk in closets frame the door to the bathroom.

Bathroom.Body: We shared this bathroom. Two sinks sit on either side of a large bathtub in the corner. The shower is around the corner, metal rims turning white from constant exposure to water.
